content: Active agentic retrieval search agents can be redesigned to draw on the biological definition of agents to dynamically self-replicate like infectious biological agents, generating targeted search sub-agents that spread through interconnected knowledge networks to retrieve niche, hard-to-index information that generic single search agents cannot reach. This framework reimagines search agents not as fixed query processors, but as self-propagating active causes that adapt their population size and scope to the complexity of the information being sought.
source_topics: Active Agentic Retrieval with Search Agents
trigger_topic: Active Agentic Retrieval with Search Agents
